title: SaaS Product Architecture
subtitle: Multi-tenancy, billing, growth — the architecture decisions that define a SaaS
description: A practical course on architecting SaaS products that scale, bill correctly, and don't leak between customers. Covers multi-tenancy models, billing (subscriptions, usage, invoicing), authentication & SSO, feature flags, deployment patterns, and the operational practices that let a small team support thousands of customers. For engineers who'll build or operate a SaaS.
audience: Backend engineers, tech leads, founders building B2B / B2C SaaS. Comfortable with web apps and basic AWS / cloud concepts.
prerequisites: Built or used a web app
outcomes: Choose the right multi-tenancy model for your stage
Design billing that scales — subscriptions, usage, invoicing
Implement SSO and enterprise auth without rebuilding everything
Use feature flags for safe rollouts and per-tenant customization
Operate at scale with onboarding, support, and metrics

modules.title: Multi-tenancy models
modules.objective: How to isolate tenants — economically and safely.

lessons.title: Single DB, shared schema — the default

modules.title: Authentication for SaaS
modules.objective: From email/password to SSO without rewriting.

lessons.title: Auth services (Auth0, Clerk, WorkOS, Supabase Auth)
Deploy strategies: canary, blue/green, dark launches
Support and customer success at scale
SaaS metrics: MRR, churn, NRR, CAC
When to add SOC 2, ISO, GDPR readiness
